rulings.Case Title: THAI MING CHIU VS. CRYSTAL LEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF THAI CHIU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Plaintiff is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FREDA A. LEEPER VS. PORTIA L. OSBORNE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine & Adopt Appraised Value Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Olivia Dajee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF FREDA LEEPER , TRUSTEE OF THE FREDA A. LEEPER TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 27, 2016 Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine & Adopt Appraised Value Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Olivia Dajee is continued to March 28, 2025. The Court will conduct a hearing pursuant to CCP 874.316(f) at that time. If parties wish the Court to consider any evidence other than the appraisal filed on January 21, 2025, parties must submit such evidence to the Court no later than March 14, 2025. No further briefing is allowed. The filings shall be limited to objections pursuant to CCP 874.316(e)(3) and evidence pursuant to CCP 874.316(f). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
